titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 718

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=948 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 12:40:59.653805+00 | translation moa=949 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 12:40:59.643199+00

p.cl: Omnibus fidelibus . Willelmus de ueteri ponte Salutem .

Sciatis quod penitentia
ductus . reddo deo et sancto Cuthberto et monachis eius de coldigham terram
illam de qua contentio fuerat inter me et eosdem monachos tempore
dauid regis . quam ego iniuste super eos occupaueram . ab omni calumpnia
liberam et quietam inperpetuum per antiquam diuisam scilicet sicut riuulus decurrit
in tvedam . ita tamen ut uia pateat libera pergentibus de horuuorresdene
ad amnem .

His testibus . Ernaldus abbate de Calchou . Ricardo . Iohanne . Ramald’
monachis eius . Rabel del chene . Tomas abet . Iohanne de biliols . Ric’ .
de aldre . Vlkil de havdene . Villelmo filio Thor . Alano filio Gospatric .
To all the faithful William de Vieuxpont sends greeting.

Know that, guided by repentance, I return to God, and Saint Cuthbert and his monks at Coldingham, the land about which there had been a dispute between me and the same monks in the time of King David; [land] which I had unlawfully seized from them; free and quit from every claim for ever; by the time-honoured boundary, that is, as the burn flows into the Tweed; so that there should be a free road open for travelling from Horndean to the river.
